title: Update tests for package rename and runtime path changes
description: Rewrite test imports, mocks, CLI expectations, environment-variable expectations, and `.orchestrator` path fixtures so the test suite targets `agent_takt`, `takt`, and `.takt` while preserving the legacy env-var fallback check.
acceptance_criteria: All listed test modules import `agent_takt` instead of `codex_orchestrator`.
Tests that patch source symbols use `agent_takt...` patch targets.
Tests expecting runtime paths or config locations now reference `.takt/` where appropriate.
Coverage includes the priority order `AGENT_TAKT_RUNNER` then `ORCHESTRATOR_RUNNER`.
No remaining `codex_orchestrator` references remain in `tests/` after the update.

handoff_summary.completed: Updated 4 files with remaining `codex_orchestrator` references: replaced all `from codex_orchestrator.*` imports and `patch("codex_orchestrator.*")` targets, updated `src/codex_orchestrator/` file path strings to `src/agent_takt/`, updated `.orchestrator/` runtime path fixtures to `.takt/`, updated source file path reads in `test_config_wiring_phase3.py`, and updated patch target strings in `test_merge_safety.py` and `test_orchestrator.py`. All files pass syntax check.
handoff_summary.risks: Tests that assert against file path strings (e.g., `expected_files=["src/agent_takt/scheduler.py"]`) are correct only if the source files themselves were moved to `src/agent_takt/` by earlier beads (B-3f6ffee3). If any test reads source files directly (test_config_wiring_phase3.py reads `runner.py`, `scheduler.py`, `skills.py`), those files must exist at `src/agent_takt/` for the tests to pass.
handoff_summary.next_action: Tester agent should run the full test suite to verify all test modules import cleanly under `agent_takt` and assertions against `.takt/` paths and `AGENT_TAKT_RUNNER` env var pass.
handoff_summary.conflict_risks: No file scope conflicts expected. All changes are in `tests/` which is not claimed by other in-progress beads.
handoff_summary.design_decisions: File path strings used as test data (e.g., `expected_files=["src/agent_takt/scheduler.py"]`) were updated to match the renamed package. These are fixture data for conflict-detection tests, not real file references. The source file path reads in `test_config_wiring_phase3.py` (which read actual `.py` files to assert no hardcoded values) were updated to `src/agent_takt/` to match the renamed package location. The `ORCHESTRATOR_RUNNER` env var tests were left intact in the existing `test_legacy_orchestrator_runner_env_var` test (already added by the previous bead) and cleanup teardowns correctly pop both `AGENT_TAKT_RUNNER` and `ORCHESTRATOR_RUNNER`.
handoff_summary.test_coverage_notes: Tester should verify: (1) all 15 test modules import successfully under `agent_takt`; (2) `.takt/` path fixtures work with `RepositoryStorage` and `load_config`; (3) `AGENT_TAKT_RUNNER` env var overrides config; (4) `ORCHESTRATOR_RUNNER` legacy fallback test passes; (5) source file reads in `test_config_wiring_phase3.py` find files at `src/agent_takt/`.
handoff_summary.known_limitations: The `test_config.py::TestLoadConfigFromRepo.test_repo_config_matches_defaults` test reads from the real repo. If the repo's `.takt/config.yaml` doesn't exist yet (symlink to `.orchestrator/` may exist), `load_config` will fall back to defaults — the test already handles this case by comparing against `default_config()` values for non-tunable fields. No changes needed there.

conflict_risks: No file scope conflicts expected. All changes are in `tests/` which is not claimed by other in-progress beads.

execution_history.summary: Updated all test files to target the renamed `agent_takt` package. Four files had residual references that previous beads missed: `test_config_wiring_phase3.py` (source file path reads), `test_merge_safety.py` (patch target strings), `test_onboarding.py` (module docstring), and `test_orchestrator.py` (file path strings and `.orchestrator` runtime path references). Other files (`test_config_wiring.py`, `test_assets.py`, etc.) were already fully updated by prior beads. The legacy `ORCHESTRATOR_RUNNER` fallback test and `AGENT_TAKT_RUNNER` priority order were already in place from the previous bead.

guardrails.template_text:
# Developer Guardrails

Primary responsibility: Implement only the assigned bead inside the existing architecture and file scope.

Allowed actions:
- Modify code, configuration, or local docs required to complete the assigned bead.
- Create sub-beads for discovered follow-up work that should be handled separately.
- Verify your `{{LANGUAGE}}` changes compile and import correctly with a quick syntax check: `{{BUILD_CHECK_COMMAND}}`.

Disallowed actions:
- Run any test suite — not even a targeted `{{TEST_COMMAND}}` run or any subset. Test execution is exclusively the tester agent's responsibility. Running tests wastes the agent budget and delays the pipeline.
- Redesign unrelated architecture or broaden scope beyond the assigned bead.
- Perform final review signoff in place of a review agent.
- Silently absorb unrelated test, documentation, or planning work that should be handed off.

Required Handoff Fields:

Every developer bead **must** populate the following three fields in its output JSON. Reviewers and tester agents rely on these fields to scope their work without additional back-and-forth turns. Use `"N/A"` only when a field is genuinely inapplicable (e.g., a trivial rename with no design trade-offs); do not leave fields blank.

- **`design_decisions`** — Document non-obvious architectural or implementation choices made during this bead. Include alternatives considered and why the chosen approach was preferred. Reviewers use this to evaluate correctness without re-deriving intent. Set to `"N/A"` only if the change was purely mechanical with no meaningful trade-offs.

- **`test_coverage_notes`** — Describe what the tester agent should verify: new code paths, edge cases introduced, regression risks, and any known untested scenarios left to the tester. Set to `"N/A"` only if the bead touched no executable code paths (e.g., documentation-only changes).

- **`known_limitations`** — Call out constraints, deferred work, or known gaps in the implementation. This includes out-of-scope items deliberately left for follow-up beads and any areas where the implementation is intentionally incomplete. Set to `"N/A"` if the implementation is complete as specified with no known gaps.

Expected outputs:
- Completed or blocked JSON with concise implementation summary.
- Accurate touched files, changed files, risks, and follow-up handoff fields.
- Populated `design_decisions`, `test_coverage_notes`, and `known_limitations` fields in every response.
